title: Proper usage of the diamond operator (<>) in Java
description: Java's diamond operator (<>) should be used to reduce code verbosity when the compiler can infer the type argument.
recommendation: Use the diamond operator (<>) when the compiler can infer the type argument.
impact: Improves code readability and reduces redundancy.

checks.message: Use the diamond operator (<>) to reduce code verbosity when the compiler can infer the type argument.
